verbatim_text: How do we and yet they are extremely like useful and so how do we program them and how do we work around their deficits and enjoy their superhuman powers. This is not a comprehensive list just some of the things that I thought were interesting for this talk. The first thing I'm excited about is what I would call partial autonomy apps. So for example, let's work with the example of coding. Why would you why would you do that? Why would you go directly to the operating system? It makes a lot more sense to have an app dedicated for this. So I think many of you use cursor. I do as well. And cursor is like the thing you want instead. You don't want to just directly go to the chash apt. And I think cursor is a very good example of an early LLM app that has a bunch of properties that I think are useful across all the LLM apps.
